graphEvents.label: entity-density cache refresh has no concurrency guard
invalidateEntityDensityCache exported with no access control

title: Entity-density cache refresh has no concurrency guard — race condition during rebuild
scopeProof: All three module-level vars (cachedTerms, lastBuiltAt, lastBuildOk) lack synchronization primitive
recommendation: Add rebuildInProgress boolean lock or promise-based dedup

title: invalidateEntityDensityCache exported with no access control
scopeProof: No caller outside post-commit hooks invokes it today; exported at entity-density.ts:166
recommendation: Document as @internal; add runtime guard if ever exposed via MCP tool

title: buildRoutingQueryPlan accepts unbounded routingReasons strings for disk persistence
scopeProof: Current callers in query-router.ts pass hardcoded strings only; gap is API contract
recommendation: Validate string length ≤256 and total count ≤50 in buildRoutingQueryPlan

title: safeGetDb error swallowing loses observability — silent DB degradation
scopeProof: catch { return null; } at line 210-212 hides ALL error classes; no logging, no metric
recommendation: Add console.warn + health metric for DB access failures

reason: Parameterized query via ?.all(MIN_OUTGOING_EDGES) — constant bind, no string concat
All values are hardcoded static strings; JSON.stringify escapes any injected chars; qualityFallback.reason interpolates only Number.toFixed()
recentDecisions stores only ChannelName[][] (no query text, no trigger phrases, no user IDs)
Exposes only channel invocation rates/counts — zero paths, queries, credentials
Read synchronously per-call; single-process no concurrent mutation; no env-setter MCP tool
Cache size bounded by DB row count × tokens; reset to empty on error; 60s TTL
JSON.parse is not regex-backed; try/catch at entity-density.ts:48-57; source is DB (system-authored)
recordInvocation always records channel arrays only (no query text); by design

detail: No P0/P1 findings — all 5 specific audit questions answered definitively. Codebase security posture is strong for the reviewed surface. Four P2 defense-in-depth advisories identified.
